arms_mandatory_baselines: deepnsm-8b-published-point-estimate (~24/100 self-metric; published point, CIs unavailable — P7 RT-16 deferred minor: re-basing onto scoring DeepNSM outputs through our validator remains open, ratify-or-defer at GNG-0)

dependent_vars.definition: explications passing the composite validator bar (legality + substitutability + cross-translatability, Baartmans et al. metric definitions reused as-is)
dependent_vars.name: n_composite_pass

n_planned.detectable_alternative: reliable clearance of the 0.34 margin at n=50 needs true composite rate ≳0.54 (P8 §1.6 'tight' case, printed per the decidability lint); expected rate 0.60 declared

endpoints.test: one-sided α=0.05: Wilson 95% lower bound of the composite validator-pass score (N>=50) must exceed the DeepNSM-8B published point estimate by >= 10 points (margin substitutes for CI overlap, published CIs unavailable)
point estimate reported with both Wilson bounds (never verdict-bearing alone)

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: P1 §4b row HS-A (verbatim): Measured range: Authoring-model axis (Fable-5-class vs 8B). Reasonable extrapolation envelope: Extrapolates forward monotonically with author capability (stated assumption: authoring quality non-decreasing in model capability — supported by Law 1); says nothing about host-side scale. Literature anchor + licensing assumption: DeepNSM (Baartmans et al.) is the published floor; Law 1 (symbol-side wins) is the licensing synthesis.
kill_criterion_verbatim: P1 §4 HS-A kill criterion (verbatim): 'Not decisively better than DeepNSM-8B ⇒ the "why now" argument is weakened materially and the prospectus must say so; kernel authoring cost estimates revert to human-authoring rates in every plan document. "Decisively better" is pre-defined: the Wilson 95% lower bound of our composite validator-pass score (N≥50 explications) must exceed DeepNSM-8B's published point estimate by ≥10 points on the same metric definitions (published CIs unavailable, so the margin substitutes for CI overlap; α=0.05 one-sided).'

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py
corpus_hashes.authored-explication-set: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:g9.author (N>=50 machine-authored explications + validator outputs; ops amendment before any final-phase run)

title: G9 — authoring capability gate: Fable-class authoring in the validator loop vs DeepNSM-8B published numbers (HS-A; the why-now check)
